description: Seven-phase asset management pipeline for post-acquisition hold period monitoring. Covers onboarding, annual budgeting, quarterly performance monitoring, leasing strategy, capital planning, tenant management, and exit trigger evaluation. Coordinates 21 specialist agents across recurring and event-driven phases to produce a CONTINUE / INTERVENE / EXIT verdict each quarter. Terminal deliverable is either a confirmed hold continuation, a 90-day NOI sprint plan, or a handoff to the disposition or capital-stack pipeline.

validationRules.description: Hold period business plan must include year-by-year NOI targets, occupancy targets, and capex schedule.
At least 8 KPI targets must be defined: NOI, occupancy, DSCR, collections rate, lease renewal rate, capex spend rate, tenant satisfaction, and operating expense ratio.
All active vendor contracts must be cataloged with expiration dates and renewal terms.
All required insurance certificates must be verified as current and compliant with lender requirements.
All insurance coverage types and limits must meet or exceed lender-required minimums.
No gap in coverage between seller policy expiration and buyer policy effective date.
All major building systems (HVAC, elevator, fire/life safety, plumbing, electrical) must be cataloged with age, condition, and remaining useful life.

passConditions.description: Asset manager lead, property manager, and insurance transfer coordinator all completed successfully with no FAILED status.
Hold period business plan is complete with year-by-year targets aligned to acquisition underwriting assumptions.
All insurance policies transferred with no coverage gaps and lender compliance confirmed.

conditionalConditions.description: Systems onboarding specialist failed or produced incomplete results; building systems register is partial.
Some vendor contracts could not be located or verified; flagged for follow-up within 30 days.

failConditions.description: Insurance coverage gap exists with no binding commitment from carrier to close it within 5 business days.
One or more critical onboarding agents failed after all retry attempts.

businessPlan.description: Hold period business plan with year-by-year NOI targets, occupancy milestones, capex schedule, and debt service projections. Seeds the annual budget setup and all downstream performance monitoring.

kpiTargets.description: Baseline KPI targets including NOI, occupancy, DSCR, collections rate, lease renewal rate, capex spend rate, and operating expense ratio. Used as benchmark for quarterly performance monitoring.

vendorInventory.description: Cataloged vendor contracts with terms, expiration dates, and renewal options. Non-critical; gaps flagged for follow-up.

insuranceStatus.description: COMPLIANT, CONDITIONAL, or GAP. CONDITIONAL means minor items pending resolution. GAP blocks downstream phases.

buildingSystemsRegister.description: Building systems inventory with remaining useful life and maintenance schedule. Non-critical; feeds into capital planning.

crossAgentValidations.description: Insurance coverage types reported by property manager must match those confirmed by insurance transfer coordinator.
Debt terms in the business plan must match the acquisition closing debt terms within 0 variance.

validationRules.description: Total revenue minus total expenses must equal budgeted NOI within 0.1% tolerance.
All standard operating expense line items must be present with non-zero values or documented zero-justification.
Total planned capex for the budget year must not exceed available capex reserves plus budgeted reserve contributions.
All capex projects must be ranked by priority (critical safety, revenue impact, cost avoidance, discretionary).
Gross potential rent minus vacancy minus concessions minus collection loss must equal effective gross income within 0.5% tolerance.
Budgeted rent growth assumptions must be within 200bps of submarket forecast range.
DSCR must be calculated for each month of the budget year and must not be null.
Monthly P&I payments must reconcile to loan terms (rate, amortization, IO period) within $1 tolerance.

requiredDataKeys.description: Hold period business plan required to set budget targets and assumptions.
KPI targets required as benchmarks for budget line items.
Insurance status must be COMPLIANT or CONDITIONAL; GAP blocks budget setup.
Building systems data feeds capex planning; absence results in conservative reserve assumptions.

passConditions.description: All four budget agents completed successfully with no FAILED status.
Budgeted NOI is within 5% of the business plan Year 1 NOI target.
Projected DSCR exceeds loan covenant minimum for every month of the budget year.

conditionalConditions.description: Capex planner produced budget but building systems register was incomplete; conservative assumptions used.
Revenue modeler rent growth assumptions exceed submarket median by more than 100bps but are within 200bps ceiling.

failConditions.description: Budgeted NOI is more than 10% below business plan target, indicating fundamental underperformance risk.
Projected DSCR falls below loan covenant minimum in one or more months of the budget year.

annualBudget.description: Complete annual operating budget with revenue, expense, and NOI line items. Serves as the baseline for quarterly variance analysis.

capexBudget.description: Annual capex budget with project list, priority rankings, and reserve draw schedule.

revenueForecast.description: Revenue forecast model with rent growth assumptions, vacancy projections, and lease renewal probabilities.

debtServiceSchedule.description: Monthly debt service schedule with DSCR projections and covenant compliance status for the budget year.

budgetedNOI.description: Total budgeted NOI for the year. Primary benchmark for quarterly performance monitoring.

crossAgentValidations.description: Total revenue in the operating budget must match the revenue forecast model output within 0.5%.
NOI used in DSCR calculation must match the budgeted NOI from the operating budget.
Capex line items in operating budget must match the capex planner total within reserve contribution tolerance.

validationRules.description: Budget-to-actual variance must be calculated for every revenue and expense line item in the operating budget.
All KPI targets defined in onboarding must have actual values and variance-to-target calculations.
Reported actual NOI must equal actual EGI minus actual OpEx within 0.1% tolerance.
Delinquency rate must be calculated as total AR over 30 days divided by total monthly billings.
Any tenant with AR over 60 days or lease expiring within 6 months must appear on the watchlist.
At least 5 comparable properties must be included in the competitive position analysis with current rents and occupancy.
Current quarter DSCR must be calculated and compared to covenant minimum.
All loan covenants must be evaluated with PASS, WATCH, or BREACH status.

requiredDataKeys.description: Annual budget required as baseline for variance analysis.
Debt service schedule required for covenant watchdog DSCR calculations.
Budgeted NOI required as the primary performance benchmark.
Revenue forecast provides context for variance narratives; absence produces less detailed analysis.

passConditions.description: Performance analyst, tenant health monitor, and covenant watchdog all completed successfully.
Actual YTD NOI is within 5% of budgeted NOI for the same period.
Current DSCR exceeds loan covenant minimum with no WATCH or BREACH flags.
Physical occupancy is within 300bps of the business plan target.

conditionalConditions.description: Market pulse analyst failed or data is over 90 days old; competitive position analysis is approximate.
NOI is 5-10% below budget; underperformance is manageable but requires attention.
Delinquency rate exceeds 5% but is below the 10% intervention threshold.

failConditions.description: Actual YTD NOI is more than 10% below budget, triggering intervention.
Current DSCR is below loan covenant minimum; lender notification required.
One or more critical monitoring agents failed after all retry attempts.

quarterlyPerformanceReport.description: Full quarterly performance report with budget variance, KPI scorecard, and trend analysis.

tenantHealthDashboard.description: Tenant health status including delinquency rates, watchlist, and retention risk for each tenant.

covenantStatus.description: Covenant compliance report with current DSCR, trend, and PASS/WATCH/BREACH status per covenant.

marketPosition.description: Submarket performance and competitive position analysis. Non-critical; market pulse analyst is non-critical.

currentNOI.description: Current quarter annualized NOI. Used in exit trigger evaluation and disposition handoff.

currentOccupancy.description: Current physical occupancy rate as a decimal (0.0 to 1.0).

crossAgentValidations.description: NOI figure used in the performance report must match the NOI used in the covenant watchdog DSCR calculation.
Occupancy rate from performance analyst must be consistent with tenant count and unit count from tenant health monitor.
Collection loss in performance report should reflect the delinquency rate from tenant health monitor within 2% tolerance.

outputs: target rent schedule by unit type

validationRules.description: Target rents must be set for every unit type with supporting market comp justification.
Leasing strategy must address all leases expiring within 12 months with renewal or re-lease plan.
Negotiation playbooks must exist for at least: new lease, renewal, expansion, and downsizing scenarios.
Recommended escalation structures must include projected NOI impact over the lease term.
Sum of tenant pro-rata shares must equal 100% of the CAM pool (or documented owner-retained share).
Billed CAM minus actual CAM equals over/under recovery; sum across all tenants must net to zero within $100.

requiredDataKeys.description: Performance data needed to inform leasing strategy priorities and rent positioning.
Tenant health data required for retention-focused leasing decisions.
Current occupancy rate determines leasing urgency and concession policy.
Market position data improves rent comp accuracy; absence leads to conservative assumptions.

passConditions.description: Leasing strategist and lease negotiation specialist both completed successfully.
Every lease expiring within 12 months has a documented renewal or re-lease strategy.
Target rents are within the market rent range established by comp analysis.

conditionalConditions.description: CAM reconciliation agent failed or produced partial results; billing adjustments deferred.
Target rents exceed top of market comp range; achievable but carries vacancy risk.

failConditions.description: Leasing strategist failed to produce a strategy after all retries.
Major tenant leases (>10% of GLA) expiring within 6 months with no strategy.

leasingPlan.description: Complete leasing strategy with target rents, concession policy, and marketing plan.

negotiationPlaybooks.description: Negotiation guidance per tenant class and scenario (new, renewal, expansion, downsizing).

camReconciliation.description: CAM reconciliation results and billing adjustments. Non-critical.

projectedLeaseRevenue.description: Projected annual lease revenue based on the leasing strategy. Feeds exit trigger evaluation.

crossAgentValidations.description: Target rents from leasing strategist must match the rent assumptions in negotiation playbooks.

validationRules.description: Total capex execution plan cost must not exceed approved capex budget plus contingency reserve.
All project timelines must account for permitting, procurement, and seasonal constraints.
Energy consumption baseline must be established with EUI (Energy Use Intensity) calculated.

requiredDataKeys.description: Approved capex budget required to plan execution.
Performance data informs capex re-prioritization if financial conditions change.

passConditions.description: Capex execution manager completed with a fully budgeted and scheduled execution plan.
All capex items classified as critical safety have scheduled execution within 90 days.

conditionalConditions.description: Sustainability analyst could not complete assessment due to missing energy data.
Capex execution plan exceeds budget by 5-10%, manageable with contingency reserve.

failConditions.description: Capex execution manager failed to produce a viable plan after all retries.
Critical safety capex items cannot be funded within available reserves.

capexExecutionPlan.description: Approved capex execution plan with project list, timelines, and budget allocations.

sustainabilityBaseline.description: Energy and ESG baseline. Non-critical but informs exit positioning and investor reporting.

validationRules.description: Retention plan must address every lease expiring within 12 months with a specific retention or replacement strategy.
Retention cost-benefit must quantify TI, downtime, and re-leasing costs vs. renewal concession costs for each at-risk tenant.
All vendors with contracts above $10K annually must have a performance scorecard with response time, quality, and cost metrics.
Average resolution time, completion rate, and cost per work order must be calculated.

requiredDataKeys.description: Tenant health data required to prioritize retention efforts.
Current occupancy drives urgency of retention initiatives.
Leasing plan provides context for renewal negotiations; absence means retention operates independently.

passConditions.description: Tenant retention specialist completed with a comprehensive retention plan covering all at-risk tenants.
No tenant representing more than 10% of revenue is at imminent risk of departure without a documented response plan.

conditionalConditions.description: Operations coordinator could not complete full vendor scorecard due to data gaps.
Proposed retention concessions exceed 5% of annual revenue for the affected tenants; cost-benefit still positive.

failConditions.description: Tenant retention specialist failed to produce a plan after all retries.
Anchor tenant (>20% of revenue) has given notice or is highly likely to vacate with no viable replacement.

retentionPlan.description: Tenant retention plan with per-tenant strategies, renewal timeline, and concession budgets.

operationsReport.description: Operations efficiency report with vendor scorecards and work order analytics. Non-critical.

retentionRisk.description: LOW, MODERATE, or HIGH. Aggregate retention risk level for the property. Feeds exit trigger evaluation.

crossAgentValidations.description: Tenant satisfaction issues flagged in retention plan should correlate with service level failures in operations report.

outputs: hold vs. sell vs. refi recommendation

validationRules.description: IRR-to-date must be calculated using actual cash flows from acquisition through current quarter.
Hold/sell/refi recommendation must cite at least 3 quantitative factors and 2 qualitative factors.
All exit triggers must be evaluated: debt maturity, target return achieved, market cycle, NOI decline, and tenant risk.
Each initiative in the 90-day NOI sprint plan must have a projected dollar impact and implementation timeline.
Projected NOI recovery must be achievable within 90 days based on lease terms and market conditions.

requiredDataKeys.description: Performance data required for exit trigger evaluation and NOI intervention planning.
Covenant compliance status required to evaluate debt-related exit triggers.
Current NOI required for IRR calculation and disposition handoff.
Current occupancy required for exit trigger evaluation.
Market data improves exit timing analysis; absence leads to conservative recommendations.
Retention risk level required for tenant-related exit trigger evaluation.

passConditions.description: Exit trigger evaluator completed successfully with a clear hold/sell/refi recommendation.
Property is within 5% of business plan NOI targets, no covenant concerns, and no exit triggers activated.

conditionalConditions.description: Property underperforming but recoverable; NOI sprint plan produced with quantified recovery path.
Market conditions are ambiguous; hold recommendation is default but with elevated monitoring frequency.

failConditions.description: One or more exit triggers activated: debt maturity within 12 months with no refi path, NOI decline >15% with no recovery plan, or target return achieved and market favors sale.
Exit trigger evaluator failed after all retries; cannot determine hold/sell/refi recommendation.

exitRecommendation.description: CONTINUE, INTERVENE, or EXIT. Drives the pipeline verdict and cross-chain handoff behavior.

irrToDate.description: IRR calculated from acquisition through current quarter using actual cash flows.

noiSprintPlan.description: 90-day NOI sprint plan. Required only when recommendation is INTERVENE.

exitTriggerReason.description: Reason for EXIT recommendation if applicable. Required only when recommendation is EXIT.

holdPeriodPerformance.description: Summary of hold period performance: cumulative cash flows, NOI trajectory, occupancy trajectory, and return metrics.

crossAgentValidations.description: If exit trigger evaluator recommends INTERVENE, the NOI intervention planner must have produced a sprint plan.

verdictValues.CONTINUE: Property performing on plan; hold period continues with standard monitoring.
verdictValues.INTERVENE: Property underperforming; 90-day NOI sprint plan activated.
verdictValues.EXIT: Exit conditions met; handoff to disposition or capital stack pipeline.

crossChainHandoffs.triggerCondition: Counterpart 'acquisition' phase 'closing' verdict == PROCEED or CONDITIONAL

propertyId.description: Property identifier for hold-period asset management. Primary key for all checkpoint and log paths.

closingDate.description: Acquisition closing date in ISO 8601 format. Seeds the hold period budget calendar and reporting schedule.

acquisitionCost.description: Total all-in acquisition cost including purchase price, closing costs, and reserves. Basis for IRR and return calculations.

debtTerms.description: Final executed loan terms (rate, LTV, amortization, IO period, maturity date, covenants) for debt service modeling and covenant monitoring.

icMemo.description: Investment committee memo as reference for hold period business plan targets and investment thesis.

rentRoll.description: Validated rent roll with unit mix (or tenant schedule for SF-based assets), in-place rents, and lease expiration schedule. Seeds the initial onboarding and revenue modeling.

capexReserve.description: Capex reserve schedule from acquisition underwriting. Seeds the initial capex planning and budget setup.

holdPeriodPerformance.description: Summary of hold period financial performance including cumulative cash flows, NOI trajectory, and return metrics.

currentNOI.description: Current quarter annualized NOI for disposition pricing and buyer underwriting.

currentOccupancy.description: Current physical occupancy rate as a decimal for disposition marketing materials.

debtMaturityDate.description: Loan maturity date in ISO 8601 format. Drives disposition timeline urgency.

exitTriggerReason.description: Reason for exit recommendation (debt maturity, target return achieved, NOI decline, market timing).

marketConditions.description: Latest market position and competitive analysis for disposition pricing context.

crossChainHandoffs.triggerCondition: Phase 'reposition-trigger-evaluation' exitRecommendation == EXIT AND exitTriggerReason contains 'refinance'

propertyId.description: Property identifier for capital stack pipeline.

currentDebtTerms.description: Current loan terms for refinance comparison and sizing.

currentNOI.description: Current quarter annualized NOI for refinance underwriting and loan sizing.

refinanceReason.description: Reason for refinance (debt maturity, rate improvement, cash-out, covenant relief).

targetProceeds.description: Target refinance proceeds if cash-out is part of the strategy. Null for rate-and-term refinances.
